title: ECHO - Training module: Soil digging procedure
author: Stahl, Maximilian Carl
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Citizen Scientists will have to use the trowel provided in the ECHO toolkit to dig the soil. They will have to rake away the litter and/or the upper soil layer, including dense grass, roots, vegetation residues and stones. The hole to dig must be a 30x30x30 cm pit. While digging, all collected soil should be placed to one side without mixing it, ensuring the soil structure remains intact for the initial analysis focused on soil structure.
steps_raw: Citizen Scientists will have to use the trowel provided in the ECHO toolkit to dig the soil. They will have to rake away the litter and/or the upper soil layer, including dense grass, roots, vegetation residues and stones. The hole to dig must be a 30x30x30 cm pit. While digging, all collected soil should be placed to one side without mixing it, ensuring the soil structure remains intact for the initial analysis focused on soil structure.
